Caption: Test of calendar aging error distribution for normal distribution using the Shapiro–Wilk test [43]. With a p < 0 . 05 , the distribution of the data cannot be assumed normally distributed. The figures show the p-values of the Shapiro–Wilk test for each check-up (CU) individually, and the p-values for all data of (a) Case 1, (b) Case 2 and (c) Case 3. For calendar aging the distributions of the errors can be assumed normally distributed for all cases.

Test of cycle aging error distribution for normal distribution using the Shapiro–Wilk test [43]. With a p < 0 . 05 , the distribution of the data cannot be assumed normally distributed. The figures show the p-values of the Shapiro–Wilk test for each check-up (CU) individually, and the p-values for all data of (a) Case 1, (b) Case 2 and (c) Case 3. For cycle aging the distributions of the errors cannot be assumed normally distributed for any case.
